intro: Spirostachys venenifera, also known as the poison stinkwood, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of West Africa. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Spirostachys venenifera include alkaloids, flavonoids, and polyphenols, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
